expectations.text: all-required-files-created: outputs contains main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/notifier.py, README.md
expectations.evidence: All 5 required files present under tools/slack_notifier/
requests>=2.31.0 and slack-sdk>=3.27.0 both listed with version pins

expectations.text: main-py-is-thin: main.py is 15 lines or fewer with no business logic
expectations.evidence: main.py is 41 lines — nearly 3x the limit. Contains 8 argparse arguments and post-parse logic constructing PipelineResult dataclass with named fields.

expectations.text: class-has-type-hints: SlackNotifier class has typed __init__ and typed method signatures
expectations.evidence: Full type hints throughout. Uses str | None, dict[str, Any], proper return types.

expectations.text: readme-has-real-content: README.md has a meaningful description and usage instructions
expectations.evidence: 84-line README with Features, Usage, argument table, exit codes, library API reference

notes: Baseline generated full Slack Block Kit message builder with real implementation logic in libs/. main.py is 41 lines and constructs a PipelineResult dataclass inline. Both are well above the thin-entry-point convention.
